prerequisite_chain.summary: Linear progression within each module; Module 2 requires Module 1 completion; Module 3 requires specific Module 2 cubelets.
rules: ST-004 requires ST-001 (Module 2 feedback loop foundation)
ST-005 requires ST-002 and ST-004 (leverage points + agent control flow)
ST-006 requires ST-003 (shifting the burden foundation)

quality_gate.details: v1.0: All 6 cubelets pass quality gate. v1.1: 3 cubelets pending (ST-004, ST-005, ST-006).

lessons.title: What Is Systems Thinking?
learning_objectives: Identify the components, boundaries, and interconnections within a business system
Construct a basic system map using stocks, flows, and feedback loops
Analyze a real-world SaaS churn scenario to trace cause-and-effect relationships

artifact.description: System Explorer with diagram builder, simulation engine, and SaaS Churn example scenario

skill.name: system-map-explorer
skill.description: Guides learners through building and interpreting system maps

mcp_tool.description: Provides foundational systems thinking prompts and validation

assessment_criteria: Learner can label at least 3 stocks and 3 flows in a given system
Learner can identify at least 1 feedback loop in the SaaS Churn example
Learner can explain how a change in one variable propagates through the system

lessons.title: Where AI Fits in Your Business System
learning_objectives: Distinguish between AI as a point tool and AI as a systemic lever within business operations
Map where AI interventions create the highest leverage in a system diagram
Apply drag-and-drop AI placement to a logistics workflow and evaluate impact

artifact.description: AI Placement Workbench with drag-drop AI badges and logistics example scenario

skill.name: ai-leverage-finder
skill.description: Helps learners identify optimal AI insertion points in their business systems

mcp_tool.description: Provides AI lever analysis and placement feedback

assessment_criteria: Learner can place AI badges on at least 2 high-leverage points in the logistics example
Learner can articulate why AI at a chosen point affects the broader system
Learner can compare tool-level vs. system-level AI adoption with a concrete example

lessons.title: The SMB AI Mindset Shift
lessons.concept: SMB AI Mindset Shift (Tool Buying to System Redesign)
learning_objectives: Contrast the tool-buying mindset with a system-redesign approach to AI adoption
Simulate a workflow before and after AI-driven redesign using side-by-side comparison
Evaluate the downstream effects of redesigning a workflow versus simply adding a tool

artifact.description: Workflow Redesign Simulator with side-by-side comparison of before/after AI integration

skill.name: workflow-redesigner
skill.description: Walks learners through redesigning a workflow with AI as a system change

mcp_tool.description: Provides workflow redesign prompts and validation

assessment_criteria: Learner can describe at least 2 differences between tool-buying and system-redesign mindsets
Learner can run the simulator and identify measurable improvements in the redesigned workflow
Learner can propose a system redesign for a given SMB scenario rather than a tool purchase

lessons.title: Reinforcing Feedback Loops
learning_objectives: Define reinforcing feedback loops and distinguish them from balancing loops
Build a causal loop diagram (CLD) that captures a reinforcing loop in a business context
Predict the exponential growth or collapse behavior driven by reinforcing loops

artifact.description: Feedback Loop Builder with interactive CLD canvas for constructing and simulating loops

skill.name: reinforcing-loop-mapper
skill.description: Guides learners through identifying and mapping reinforcing feedback loops

mcp_tool.description: Provides CLD validation and reinforcing loop detection

assessment_criteria: Learner can draw a correct reinforcing loop with at least 3 variables on the CLD canvas
Learner can explain whether a given loop drives growth or decline
Learner can identify a reinforcing loop in a provided business case study

lessons.title: Leverage Points in Complex Systems
learning_objectives: Explain Donella Meadows' 12 leverage points hierarchy and rank interventions by effectiveness
Score potential interventions in a system using the leverage point framework
Recommend high-leverage interventions for an SMB scenario based on systemic analysis

artifact.description: Leverage Point Scorer with interactive Meadows hierarchy and intervention ranking

skill.name: leverage-point-analyzer
skill.description: Helps learners evaluate and rank leverage points in their systems

mcp_tool.description: Provides leverage point scoring and analysis

assessment_criteria: Learner can correctly rank at least 3 leverage points from the Meadows hierarchy
Learner can score a set of proposed interventions and justify the ranking
Learner can identify the highest-leverage intervention in a given SMB scenario

lessons.title: Shifting the Burden Archetype
learning_objectives: Recognize the Shifting the Burden archetype and its symptomatic vs. fundamental solution structure
Simulate burden-shifting dynamics across three scenarios and observe long-term degradation
Design an intervention that addresses the fundamental solution rather than the symptomatic fix

artifact.description: Burden Shift Simulator with 3 interactive scenarios demonstrating the archetype

skill.name: burden-shift-detector
skill.description: Helps learners detect and diagnose shifting-the-burden patterns in organizations

mcp_tool.description: Provides burden-shift pattern detection and scenario analysis

assessment_criteria: Learner can diagram the Shifting the Burden archetype with symptomatic and fundamental loops
Learner can run all 3 simulator scenarios and explain why the symptomatic fix fails long-term
Learner can propose a fundamental solution for a given burden-shifting case

lessons.title: Agent Feedback Loops
learning_objectives: Map an agent architecture as a causal loop diagram using agent-specific node vocabulary
Identify reinforcing and balancing feedback loops within agent control flows
Score loop severity and predict runaway behavior (retry storms, cost spirals)
Design interventions to break harmful agent feedback loops

artifact.description: Agent CLD Builder with agent-specific nodes, auto-detected feedback loops, and severity scoring

skill.name: agent-feedback-analyzer
skill.description: Guides learners through mapping and analyzing agent architecture feedback loops

mcp_tool.description: Accepts agent components and links, returns loop classification with severity scores

assessment_criteria: Learner can place at least 3 agent-specific nodes and connect them with causal links
Learner can identify at least 1 reinforcing loop in the retry storm worked example
Learner can predict loop behavior before revealing algorithmic analysis

lessons.title: Tool Orchestration as System Design
learning_objectives: Input MCP tools and their dependencies to build a tool stack dependency graph
Identify redundancy, coupling, and blast radius across a tool stack
Score tool interventions (add, remove, refactor) using Meadows hierarchy
Evaluate tool stack health using complexity, redundancy, and brittleness metrics

artifact.description: Tool Stack Analyzer with dependency graph, health scoring, and Meadows-based intervention ranking

skill.name: tool-stack-analyzer
skill.description: Guides learners through auditing and analyzing MCP tool stacks as systems

mcp_tool.description: Accepts tool list, dependencies, and interventions, returns health scores and refactor recommendations

assessment_criteria: Learner can input at least 3 tools with dependencies and generate a dependency graph
Learner can identify the highest blast-radius tool in the worked example
Learner can score at least 2 interventions using Meadows hierarchy and justify the ranking

lessons.title: Shifting the Burden in Automation
learning_objectives: Recognize the shifting-the-burden archetype in AI automation contexts
Play a 12-round scenario-based decision game to experience automation debt accumulation
Classify automation responses as quick-fix, fundamental investment, or transition strategy
Design transition plans that maintain symptomatic fixes while building fundamental automation solutions

artifact.description: Automation Debt Simulator with 3 AI scenarios, 12-round decision game, and post-game debt diagnosis

skill.name: automation-debt-detector
skill.description: Guides learners through detecting and diagnosing automation debt using shifting-the-burden archetype

mcp_tool.description: Accepts automation layers and fundamental solution, identifies burden shift patterns with loop analysis

assessment_criteria: Learner can complete at least 1 of the 3 scenarios and score B or higher
Learner can identify the erosion channel in their chosen scenario
Learner can design a transition strategy for a given automation debt case
